pr.title: Matched all ftKoopa inline asm functions
pr.author: Tri-Wing

summary: Converted the remaining inline assembly in Bowser/ftKoopa into C, backed by expanded Koopa attribute and fighter struct definitions. The PR removed large asm blocks from ftkoopa.c, matched lifecycle and helper routines such as ftKoopa_OnDeath, ftKoopa_OnLoad, func_80132B3C, and func_80132E30, and exposed offsets needed for matching in ftkoopa.h and fighter.h. There was no PR body or review discussion; the only recorded review was an approval.
what_changed: Replaced multiple ftKoopa inline asm functions with C implementations, including ftKoopa_OnDeath, func_80132A64, func_80132A84, ftKoopa_OnLoad, func_80132B38, func_80132B3C, func_80132C1C, func_80132C64, func_80132CAC, func_80132D00, and func_80132E30.
Converted asm copy loops over Koopa special attributes into direct struct copies such as *sA2 = *ext_attr, then assigned x2D4_specialAttributes to the copied x2D8_specialAttributes2 storage.
Expanded ftKoopaAttributes from a partially padded struct with unk34 through unk4C into a mostly offset-named layout from x0 through x9C, including u32 fields at x4, x20, x2C, and unk50.
Added ftKoopaVars with two f32 fields, used by ftKoopa_OnDeath as an overlay on Fighter offset x222C for values loaded from Koopa attributes x10 and x18.
Changed Koopa attribute accessors from unk34/unk38/unk3C/unk40/unk44/unk48/unk4C to offset-style names x34/x38/x3C/x40/x44/x48/x4C.
Added Fighter fields needed by the matched C: x2C_facing_direction, x18B0, and x1A58, replacing portions of filler while preserving offsets.
Expressed item-state logic in func_80132B3C as a switch over func_8026B320(ft->x1974_heldItem), mapping cases 1, 2, 3, and 4 to func_80070FB4 argument values 1, 0, 2, and 3 respectively.
Matched held-item guard helpers by testing func_8026B2B4(ft->x1974_heldItem) == 0 before calling func_80070CC4 or func_80070C48.
Matched func_80132E30 by using x2210_ThrowFlags.b4, flipping x2C_facing_direction when set, writing x234C_stateVar4, and clearing x2200_ftcmd_var0 after calls involving x1A58.
smart_moves: Recognized the repeated mtctr 0x14 / 8-byte copy loop as a full ftKoopaAttributes struct copy, which simplified several functions and justified expanding the header layout.
Introduced only the Fighter fields needed at exact offsets instead of overhauling the entire Fighter struct, minimizing unrelated header churn while enabling C to address known offsets directly.
Used existing bitfield names where available, such as x2226_flag.bits.b1 and x2210_ThrowFlags.b4, to replace rlwimi/rlwinm bit manipulation from the asm.
Preserved low-level offset-based naming for uncertain fields, which kept the match grounded without inventing semantic names for Koopa attributes whose meanings were not documented.
Translated branch-table-style item logic into a compact switch, making the control flow understandable while retaining the observed case-to-argument mapping.
Kept small matching artifacts visible in the C, such as unused locals in some functions, rather than over-cleaning code that may have been necessary for code generation.
decomp_lessons: When an asm loop copies a fixed number of words from ext_attr into per-fighter special attributes, check whether the loop size corresponds exactly to a character attribute struct and replace it with struct assignment.
For fighter character files, matching often depends on adding precise Fighter fields at offsets referenced by asm rather than relying on raw filler or casts.
Offset-style names like x34, x4C, and x9C are preferable to arbitrary unk names when the field offset is known but the semantic purpose is not.
Bitfield definitions can turn rlwimi/rlwinm sequences into straightforward C assignments and tests, but the exact bit index must come from the instruction behavior and existing struct layout.
Switch statements can naturally match compare/branch ladders over small integer item states, especially when cases map to repeated calls with only one changing argument.
Do not remove apparently unused locals automatically in decomp work; their presence may be intentional for register allocation or stack/codegen matching unless reviewers later request cleanup.
naming_conventions: Character attribute structs use the ftCharacterAttributes pattern: ftKoopaAttributes.
Unknown but offset-known fields in structs are named with hexadecimal offset-style members such as x0, x10, x4C, and x9C.
Fighter fields follow offset-prefixed names with limited semantic suffixes where known, e.g. x2C_facing_direction, x1974_heldItem, x18B0, and x1A58.
Per-character runtime variable overlays can be named ftKoopaVars and addressed by casting a known Fighter offset such as &ft->x222C.
Existing generic function symbols remained unnamed where no semantic name was established, e.g. func_80132B3C, func_8026B2B4, and func_80070FB4.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced mtctr 0x14 plus paired lwz/stw copy loop with a single ftKoopaAttributes struct assignment; 0x14 iterations of 8 bytes implies a 0xA0-byte attribute struct.
Mapped offset 0x2D8 to x2D8_specialAttributes2 as the destination copy buffer and 0x2D4 to x2D4_specialAttributes as the active attribute pointer.
Replaced a trivial branch wrapper at func_80132A64 with a direct call to func_80134D78(gobj) followed by return.
Matched a no-op asm function at func_80132B38 as an empty void function returning immediately.
Translated item status tests around func_8026B2B4 and func_8026B320 into nested if and switch constructs.
Translated throw flag handling in func_80132E30 into a BOOL flag_set temporary, clearing x2210_ThrowFlags.b4 before negating x2C_facing_direction and setting x234C_stateVar4.
Translated asm loads/stores at Fighter offsets 0x18B0, 0x222C, 0x2230, 0x2200, and 0x1A58 into typed field accesses or casts.

review_feedback.notes: No review comments or rationale were present in the slice.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename ftKoopaAttributes fields x34 through x9C to semantic names.
Find later changes to Fighter x1A58 and whether it receives a semantic field name.
Search for other character PRs replacing mtctr 0x14 attribute copy loops with struct assignment.
Check whether unused locals in ftKoopa_OnDeath and ftKoopa_OnLoad were later cleaned up or retained for matching.
Find later PRs that name func_8026B2B4, func_8026B320, or func_80070FB4 to clarify the item-state logic.
